title: Sneaker company Allbirds plans to pivot to AI. Yes, AI.
author: New York Times Service
bullets: Business After selling its business for $39 million last month, the company said it planned to buy powerful computer chips and rebrand itself NewBird AI.
The shoe company Allbirds announced on Wednesday that it would “pivot its business” to artificial intelligence.

title: Don’t want an $80 train ticket to the World Cup? How about a $95 bus ticket?
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: World Cup World Cup officials have launched the Boston Stadium Express Bus Service, a convenient but pricey option for match attendees.
World Cup organizers are offering a premium bus service to Boston Stadium, with a premium price to match.
Danielle Parhizkaran/The Boston Globe FIFA World Cup organizers have announced that bus service will be available in Massachusetts during this summer’s match days — but it comes at a premium price.

title: Plum Island’s iconic Pink House, demolished last year, memorialized with new sign

bullets: Local News With an artist's rendering, the new sign reads “honoring 100 years of memories.
